basic_info.title: World of Final Fantasy
basic_info.review: World of Final Fantasy is a charming spin-off featuring chibi versions of classic FF characters. The PS4/PS5 version offers the best experience with Maxima DLC adding significant content including new mirages, dungeons, and harder difficulty modes.

alternative_names: ワールド オブ ファイナルファンタジー (World of Final Fantasy)

play_today.details: The definitive version with all Maxima DLC content including new mirages, characters, dungeons, and harder difficulty modes. Best performance on PS4 Pro or PS5.
PC version with WOFFFix mod by Lyall adds ultrawide support and unlocks FPS beyond 30fps. Includes all Maxima content.

recommended_patches.name: WOFFFix Mod
recommended_patches.description: Ultrawidescreen mod that adds support for custom resolutions and unlocks FPS beyond 30fps.

recommended_patches.name: Ultimate Engine Tweaks
recommended_patches.description: Eliminates stutters, boosts FPS, and reduces input lag without sacrificing visual quality.

play_today.details: Portable version with all Maxima content. Performance is generally good but slightly inferior to PS4 version.
Original handheld release. Good experience but lacks Maxima DLC content unless purchased separately.

optimal_settings: Enable GPU emulation for better performance
Set resolution scaling to 2x or higher

play_today.details: Console version with all Maxima content. Performance comparable to PS4 but lacks PlayStation-specific features.

description.synopsis: Twins Lann and Reyna discover they can summon Chocobos and other Final Fantasy characters as 'Mirages' in a world where classic FF characters exist as collectible creatures.
key_features: Chibi-style character designs from across the Final Fantasy series
Maxima DLC adds new content including harder difficulties
Crossover featuring characters from multiple FF games
description.long_description: World of Final Fantasy, developed by Square Enix and directed by Koichi Ishii, is a unique spin-off that reimagines classic Final Fantasy characters in adorable chibi form. Released on PS Vita in October 2016 and expanded to other platforms as Maxima in November 2017/March 2018, the game features an innovative Mirage system where players collect, fuse, and battle with iconic FF characters. The Maxima version adds significant content including new Mirages (characters), additional dungeons, harder difficulty modes, and quality-of-life improvements like New Game+ with inventory retention. Digital Foundry noted that while charming, the PS Vita version had performance limitations compared to the PS4/PC versions. The game received praise for its art style, music, and nostalgic appeal, though some critics found it too casual for traditional FF fans.
legacy_and_impact: Introduced chibi-style reimagining of classic Final Fantasy characters
Created unique Mirage collection/fusion gameplay mechanics
Served as a bridge between mainline FF games and spin-offs
Maxima DLC set new standards for post-launch content in mobile-first titles
Featured crossover appeal with over 50+ recognizable FF characters
